titleInfo.title: A Discussion of Illustrations of Hell

mods.abstract: Ilustrations of Hell exist since the sixth century. Wu Daozi 吳道子 painted them. 益州名畫錄 records that many temples in Chengdu had wall paintings of hell scenes. Those are based on several sutras. Hu treats Sichuan cave temples with sculpted hell scenes. Anyue Yuanjue Dong 圓覺洞, niche 80 and 84. Niche 80 has an inscription dated to 1097 (宋紹聖四年), but was made even earlier, 934-965. Niche 84 is probably from the same time, as niches 80-84 appear to have been made at the same time. From the middle of the fifth century we have depictions of hell scenes in Kizil (Thousand Buddha Cave Qianfo Dong). From the Sui there is the cosmological Buddha in the Freer Gallery in Washington. At Longmen in Binyang Dong, sixth century, 輪回圖. Dazu, Beishan, niche 253: Dizang, Guanyin, Ten Kings, from the Five Dynasties. Dazu 石篆石第9號龕: Dizang and the Ten Kings. Dazu Baoding Shan niche 20.
